html_content:
INSIGHT: The Single Most Important Thing
68.85% component commonality across S1 Pro, S1 Air, and S1 X+ enabled Ola to strip down a premium scooter into a budget variant at minimal engineering cost — the unsung structural enabler of the margin paradox.

Generation 2 Platform

Category: Core Technology
Status: In Production (since July 2023)

---

Overview

The Generation 2 platform is the common EV architecture underpinning all current Ola S1 scooter models. It represents a significant improvement over the Generation 1 platform.

---

Key Specifications (Ola S1 Pro Comparison)

| Feature | Gen 1 | Gen 2 |
|---------|-------|-------|
| Acceleration | Baseline | Enhanced |
| Top Speed | Baseline | Improved |
| Range | Baseline | Extended |
| Motor Power | Baseline | Higher |
| Battery Performance | Baseline | Better |

---

Component Commonality

68.85% of components are common across three scooter models built on Gen 2:


  • Ola S1 Pro

  • Ola S1 Air

  • Ola S1 X+

Benefits of Platform Approach:

1. Faster product development — New models built from common architecture 2. Lower BOM cost — Shared components increase purchasing scale 3. Reduced R&D spend per model — Amortize development across multiple products 4. Manufacturing efficiency — Common assembly processes, tooling 5. Faster time to market — 5 new scooter models announced after first product

---

In-House Designed Components

  • Electric powertrain (modular design)
  • Battery pack with BMS (varies in size by model)
  • Motors
  • Power electronics modules
  • Vehicle frames (manufacturing started Aug 2023)

---

Platform Replication Strategy

Ola plans to apply the same platform approach to:


  • Motorcycles — 4 models announced

  • Future Gen 3+ — continued evolution

---

Data Sources

DATA: Source: PRODUCT_MIX_QUARTERLY.csv
S1 X (built on Gen 2 platform) grew from 15% to 75% of sales mix — the platform's cost structure made the mass-market push viable. Gross profit per unit rose 26% (₹24,300 → ₹30,527).
DATA: Source: GIGAFACTORY_MILESTONES.csv
BOM reduction from 0% (Mar 2024) to 30% (Apr 2026) was accelerated by Gen 2 platform commonality compounding with Gigafactory cell savings from $130/kWh to $80/kWh.
DATA: Source: EV_SEGMENT_COMPARISON.csv
Ola's 36% gross margin (Q4 FY26) exceeds Bajaj EV margins — the platform strategy is a structural advantage that TVS/Bajaj with model-specific designs cannot quickly replicate.
DATA: Source: GIGAFACTORY_MILESTONES.csv
The platform approach extends beyond scooters: 4 motorcycle models (Roadster etc.) planned on scalable architecture, and Gen 3+ evolution targeting $70/kWh cell cost by Mar 2028.
DATA: Source: market/SERVICE_NETWORK_COMPARISON.csv
Ather Energy operates 700 experience centers with purpose-built EV service infrastructure and the Ather Grid (2,000+ charging points). While smaller than legacy networks, Ather's network is purpose-built for EVs, not adapted from ICE service centers.
Causal chain: Purpose-built EV service eliminates the training gap seen when ICE mechanics handle high-voltage EV systems. Ather's 2-3 day turnaround (better than Ola's 3-15 but worse than legacy's 1-2) reflects this trade-off
Cells: Row 5 (Ather): Total_Dealer_Network = 700 experience centers, Charging_Infrastructure = Ather Grid (2000+ points)
DATA: Source: financial_mda.txt
68.85% of components were common across S1 Pro, S1 Air, and S1 X+ models as of Oct 2023, demonstrating the platform approach. Gen 2 platform started manufacturing Jul 2023 with enhanced specs (acceleration, speed, range, motor power, battery).
Causal chain: High component commonality across models reduced per-unit costs and sped up new model development, enabling the rapid S1 X launch.
Cells: Page 318: Platform commonality and Gen 2 details
DATA: Source: ola_ipo/industry_overview_business.txt
Ola's Generation 2 platform is the base for S1 Pro, S1 Air, S1 X+, S1 X (2 kWh), and S1 X (3 kWh) models. The platform enhances acceleration, speed, range, motor power, and battery performance versus the Generation 1 platform. It features a modular electric powertrain with a shared battery pack, BMS, motor, and power electronics module adaptable across models.
Context: Pages 161-162, 165, Our Business
DATA: Source: ola_ipo/industry_overview_business.txt
Platform approach enables rapid product development: Ola delivered 4 products and announced 6 new products since first product announcement in August 2021. The modular electric powertrain (battery pack + BMS + motor + power electronics) and centralized electronics architecture with ECU/HMI can be adapted for different models, reducing estimated product development costs and time to market.
Context: Pages 161, 165, Our Business
DATA: Source: ola_ipo/industry_overview_business.txt
Gen 2 platform is designed with flexible manufacturing lines adaptable to different product offerings. All Gen 2 products are powered by MoveOS (in-house operating system). MoveOS version 4 launched as beta in September 2023 with features: Ola Maps navigation, call filter, find my scooter, geofencing, time fencing, anti-theft alert, fall detection, hill hold, auto turn-off indicators, ride journal, and energy insights.
Context: Page 162, Our Business
DATA: Source: ola_ipo/industry_overview_business.txt
Platform approach extends beyond scooters: Ola plans to replicate the same platform strategy for its motorcycle range (4 models: Diamondhead, Adventure, Roadster, Cruiser). The 68.85% component commonality across S1 Pro, S1 Air, and S1 X+ includes the same battery pack across all three models, demonstrating the scalability of the common architecture.
Context: Pages 162, 165, Our Business
DATA: Source: ola_ipo/industry_overview_business.txt
Ola's R&D team (974 employees including 60 PhD holders across India, UK, US) works on five interconnected technology pillars: (a) MoveOS software, (b) electronics, (c) motor and drivetrain, (d) cells and battery packs, and (e) manufacturing technology. The BIC (Battery Innovation Centre) in Bengaluru develops cell technology and manufacturing processes for the Gigafactory.
Context: Pages 162, 164, Our Business
DATA: Source: ola_ipo/industry_overview_business.txt
First EV model (Ola S1 Pro) delivered December 2021. Ola S1 delivered September 2022. Ola S1 X+ delivered December 2023. Ola S1 X (2 kWh) and (3 kWh) announced for delivery H1 FY2025. Motorcycles (4 models) announced for H1 FY2026. All existing models charge on the same charging network.
Context: Page 160, Our Business

Connections

Causes: Technology/R&D Investment

Effects: Strategy/Product_Mix_Cannibalization, Strategy/Operating Leverage

Amplifiers: Strategy/Backward Integration, Technology/Gigafactory_Updates

Linked Nodes: Technology/MoveOS, Strategy/EV Hub Tamil Nadu
